content.full_text:
Kevin & Christina Jeffries

December 17, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 900
Sacramento, CA 95815

RE: Conflict of Interest question

I was recently elected to the Elsinore Valley Municipal Water
District (EVMWD) as the Director of Division-3, and I'm in need
of FPPC conflict of interest direction.

In 1985 EVMWD entered into various local, state and federal
financial agreements to enhance lake stabilization at Lake
Elsinore. Currently the seven principal agencies involved are:
the Lake Elsinore Management Authority (LEMA) projects and
EVMWD, City of Lake Elsinore, Redevelopment Agency of the City
of Lake Elsinore, State Dept. of Parks & Recreation, Riverside
County, Riverside County Flood Control District and the Santa
Ana Watershed Project Authority.

The overall goals of the LEMA agency is to refine revenues and
costs and to develop and maintain interagency agreements on:
Public safety, lake patrol, water replenishment, wildlife wet-
lands costs, resident permits, boat lake use & parking fees,
tax allocation, lake water supply, lake water levels, wetlands
water supply, lake drainage, irrigation facilities, water
control, levee & channel maintenance, project land acquisition,
construction costs and interagency revenue support.

Because EVMWD is the lead agency for the LEMA project, I as a
EVMWD Director anticipate being called upon, starting January
7th 1991, to participate in various decisions affecting the
lake management project as listed above.

My question of a conflict of interest issue revolves around my
50% ownership (my mom is the other 50% owner) of lake front
parcels that was previously held by my grandfather for many
years. After discussing this issue with EVMWD legal counsel,
I feel that I should seek your direction as to any limitations
on my ability to participate in the projects decisions.

Even though my percentage of overall ownership interest may
not be distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
as indicated on the next page, I'm uncomfortable with assuming
I have the right to vote on such specific issues as:

(1. Determining if the future lake water level should
    encroach onto all lake front parcels.

(2. Any LEMA acquisition of parcels similar to those I own.

(3. Resident permit costs.


17666 Grand Avenue, Lake Elsinore CA 92330

Kevin & Christina Jeffries

FPPC - CONT.

RE: Conflict of Interest question
Percentage of overall ownership statistics

1. Approximate total # of lake front properties:

A. Unincorporated area single lots:                166
B. Unincorporated area lots with
   association ownership:                    (3)
   1. Kilman Orchard Tract Lots (assoc):     107
   2. Wadsell Tract Lots (assoc):             60
   3. Vista Del Lago Tract (share holders): 269

C. Incorporated area single lots:             212*
   *includes 4 large mobile home/RV parks
D. Incorporated area lots with
   association ownership:                    (1)
   1. Serena Shores Tract Lots (assoc):      28

2. Total number of potential lake front interest lots: 842

3. My interest in lake front lots (50% ownership):
   A. Unincorporated area lots:    3
      (1 is my primary residence. 1 grandfathers residence.
       1 vacant).
   B. Incorporated area lots in a association that
      has one lake front lot:    4

4. Percentage of my interest in the 842 lots above: %  .0083
   (not reflecting 50% ownership)

5. Total percentage of the 842 lots I will represent
   in my EVMWD division (654):                %  77.67

6. Proposed primary benefit zone acreage total:
   (covers more than just lake front property)

   A. Area inside LEMA benefit Boundary:      7,500.00

   B. Total acreage of my interest in boundary:  (8.24)
      (not reflecting only 50% ownership)

   C. My total primary benefit zone percentage:  %  .0010
      (not reflecting only 50% ownership)

The above figures are based on the following sources:
1. My property tax bills.
2. Parcel maps (county & city).
3. Verbal discussion with Vista Del Lago Assoc. President
4. 1989 Lake Elsinore Management Project Financing Plan

I appreciate your time and assistance in this matter.

                                        Kevin D. Jeffries
                                        714-678-4730

17666 Grand Avenue, Lake Elsinore CA 92330
